prompt.role: You are a senior software engineer specializing in API documentation, developer experience, and observability for Rust applications.

task.title: API Documentation and Developer Experience
task.description: Implement API documentation using OpenAPI/Swagger, enhance logging with structured JSON output, and improve developer experience with hot reload and environment configuration. Create comprehensive documentation that makes the API self-documenting and easy to explore.

technical_specifications.spec: Add utoipa and utoipa-swagger-ui for OpenAPI documentation
Create documentation module with OpenAPI specification
Annotate all models with ToSchema derive and examples
Document all route handlers with utoipa::path macro
Implement structured JSON logging with tracing-bunyan-formatter
Create hot reload development script with cargo-watch

prompt.implementation_details:
1. OpenAPI Documentation Setup: - Add utoipa dependencies with axum feature - Create src/docs.rs with ApiDoc struct - Define OpenAPI metadata (title, version, description) - Configure server URLs for different environments - Define tags for endpoint grouping
2. Model Annotations: - Add ToSchema derive to all models - Provide realistic example values - Include field descriptions - Specify format constraints (email, date-time) - Document validation rules
3. Route Documentation: - Annotate each handler with utoipa::path - Document all status codes - Provide request/response schemas - Include parameter descriptions - Add security requirements
4. Swagger UI Integration: - Configure SwaggerUi in main.rs - Serve at /swagger-ui path - Expose OpenAPI JSON at /api-docs/openapi.json - Enable try-it-out functionality - Support multiple server environments
5. Structured Logging: - Create src/logging.rs module - Implement Bunyan JSON formatter - Configure tracing subscriber - Support environment-based log levels - Include correlation ID support
6. Developer Experience: - Create scripts/dev.sh for development - Install and use cargo-watch - Auto-start PostgreSQL with docker-compose - Auto-run migrations - Display helpful startup messages
7. Environment Configuration: - Create comprehensive .env.example - Document all environment variables - Provide sensible defaults - Include production guidance - Separate dev/prod configurations

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Swagger UI accessible and fully functional at /swagger-ui
All API endpoints documented with descriptions and examples
Interactive API testing works through Swagger UI
JSON structured logs output in correct format
Hot reload detects file changes and restarts automatically
Environment variables properly loaded from .env file
Development script automates database and migration setup
All models have complete schema documentation
Error responses documented for all endpoints
No sensitive data exposed in logs or documentation

prompt.test_strategy:
Documentation and Developer Experience Testing:
1. Swagger UI Testing: - Navigate to http://localhost:3000/swagger-ui - Verify all endpoints listed - Test "Try it out" for each endpoint - Validate example requests work - Check response schemas accurate
2. Documentation Quality: - Verify all fields have descriptions - Check example values are realistic - Ensure error responses documented - Validate OpenAPI spec structure - Test schema generation
3. Logging Verification: - Check JSON format output - Test log level filtering - Verify no sensitive data logged - Measure performance impact - Test correlation ID propagation
4. Developer Workflow: - Run ./scripts/dev.sh successfully - Modify code and verify hot reload - Test environment variable loading - Verify database auto-connection - Check migration execution
5. Performance Testing: - Swagger UI load time < 2 seconds - Documentation generation < 100ms - Logging overhead < 1ms - Hot reload trigger < 1 second - Build time acceptable
Security validation: - No real user data in examples - No hardcoded credentials - Logs properly sanitized - Environment variables secure
prompt.instructions:
Think step-by-step about creating excellent API documentation and developer experience. Consider both the needs of API consumers and developers working on the project.
Start by setting up the OpenAPI documentation infrastructure with utoipa. Create a well-organized documentation module that defines the API specification with complete metadata.
Carefully annotate all models with schema information, providing helpful descriptions and realistic examples. Each field should be documented with its purpose and constraints.
Document every API endpoint comprehensively, including all possible responses, error conditions, and examples. Make the documentation interactive and useful for testing.
Configure Swagger UI to provide an excellent exploration experience. Users should be able to understand and test the API without external tools.
Implement structured logging that provides valuable debugging information without compromising security. Use JSON format for easy parsing and analysis.
Create developer tools that make working on the project a pleasure. The hot reload setup should be fast and reliable, with clear feedback on what's happening.
Design the environment configuration to be self-documenting and secure. Provide clear examples without exposing sensitive information.
Test everything thoroughly - the documentation should be accurate, the logging should be helpful, and the developer experience should be smooth.
Remember that good documentation is a feature, not an afterthought. It should make the API self-explanatory and easy to integrate with.
